description:
Three gaps in SPEC-clone-layout.md surfaced while landing the bottom-layer slice of bl-77cb (commit f60262d, vendored SHA-256 + clone-identity). Each is a small SPEC edit; bundling them avoids three docs-only balls.

Filed as a sibling rather than a dep of bl-77cb so the implementation can move forward in parallel — the open questions affect later slices of Phase 1, not the slice that's already landed. The next agent on bl-77cb should pick this up before deciding what stealth/no-git mode looks like under XDG.

## 1. §4 vs §10 — lowercase scope disagreement

§4 prose says "lowercased scheme/host"; the §10 hand-operable sed pipeline lowercases the entire post-strip string via `tr 'A-Z' 'a-z'`. The implementation in `src/clone_identity.rs` follows §10 (broader rule) and notes the discrepancy in its doc-comment. SPEC §4 should be amended to match §10 — either:
- Update §4 to say "lowercased" (whole string), matching §10; or
- Update §10 to use a narrower `tr` that only touches the host segment, matching §4.

The first option is preferred — it's what's already shipped in the binary, and the §10 sequence is named in §10 as "must remain valid for the life of the design." Narrowing §10 would require a code change in clone-identity and a re-pin of the §14.2 golden vectors.

## 2. `<path-hash>` breadcrumb file is unnamed

§8 references "recorded clone path" for `bl doctor`'s moved-clone detection but never names the file or its schema. Phase 3 (bl-05e5) gates on this — the doctor read path needs to know what to read. The breadcrumb should be written by Phase 1's materialization (so it exists before doctor needs it), and Phase 3's doctor enrichment reads it.

Suggested:

- Path: `~/.local/state/balls/<origin-key>/<path-hash>/clone-path.txt`
- Contents: one line, `<absolute git-dir path>\t<hostname>`. Tab-separated so a `cut` does the right thing.
- Hostname per SPEC §12's NFS-shared-`$HOME` consideration (per-clone needs per-host scoping; doctor's moved-clone report must not flag cross-host hashes as "moved").

The SPEC should add a §8.1 (or extend §7 step 7) naming the file, schema, and the write+read invariant.

## 3. Stealth / no-git mode under XDG is undefined

The existing `bl init --tasks-dir /path` no-git mode works against a clone that has no `origin` URL. §4 ("a pre-remote clone … cannot derive `<origin-key>`") explicitly punts: "This SPEC does not engineer around that: the bootstrap convention (§5) requires `origin` to exist."

But stealth/no-git is a shipped feature (bl-e0f8); removing it would be a breaking change that this SPEC does not call out. Two possible resolutions:

- **Synthetic `<origin-key>`** — derive from the canonicalized absolute tasks-dir path when `origin` is missing. Keeps no-git mode working; one extra branch in `Store::discover`'s key derivation. Cost: a hand-following user of §10 hits an `origin`-missing case that the SPEC doesn't cover.
- **Stealth keeps the legacy in-tree layout forever** — the XDG pivot only applies to clones that have an `origin`. Cleanly bounded; matches the SPEC's silence. Cost: dual-read code in `Store::discover` becomes permanent for the stealth branch (Phase 4's bump never gates it out).

This is a design call the user should make. The SPEC must then either §4 + §10 cover the synthetic-key case, or add a §13 non-goal explicitly stating "no-git/stealth clones keep the in-tree layout; XDG applies only when `origin` is set."

## Why one ball, not three

Each individual edit is small (a few paragraphs) and the three are concentrated in SPEC §4, §8, and §13. Splitting them produces three sub-1-day docs balls with overlapping context loading. The unifying thread — "what the implementation actually needs from the SPEC text" — is the same.

## Out of scope

- No code changes. If the §4/§10 resolution requires re-pinning golden vectors, that lands with the SPEC change as part of this ball's commit, but no other implementation files are touched.
- No phase rescoping. The four implementation phases (bl-77cb, bl-717e, bl-05e5, bl-a84c) remain as filed. This ball just removes ambiguity from the spec they implement against.
title: SPEC-clone-layout: resolve open questions surfaced while implementing Phase 1
